mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering rapid equity sell-offs within the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day forward returns in >50% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output, which depresses corporate profit margins and yields negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods following INDPRO declines, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with restrictive policy rates creates stagflationary pressure that rapidly erodes equity valuations, causing negative returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of cases when both thresholds are met, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer spending capacity, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods following payroll increases, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in real GDP signals an economic downturn that reduces corporate revenue expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts, or the predictive accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment indicates a tightening labor market that boosts wage pressures and corporate pricing power, driving positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in >55% of instances, or the interaction term fails to achieve statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.
